_comment: Queue for the Blog Post Writer cron. The cron pulls the first item with status 'pending', writes the post, sets status to 'published' with the commit hash, and moves on. Hub-and-spoke clusters: each post links back to its hub for SEO topic-cluster authority.

_format.title: H1 / page title
_format.hub: URL of the pillar/hub page this post links back to
_format.angle: What makes this post unique vs the hub
_format.heroImage: Path under /public/blog (relative). Used as og:image and inline hero.
_format.heroImageAlt: Descriptive alt text using primary keyword (AISO + a11y)

queue.title: Cornerstone PM vs AccuLynx: Home Builder Software Compared
queue.metaDescription: AccuLynx is the #1 roofing CRM, but home builders need more. Compare AccuLynx vs Cornerstone PM on floorplans, design center, and multi-community workflows.
queue.angle: AccuLynx is unambiguously a roofing-only CRM ($250/mo Essential plan, ABC Supply / QXO / SRS direct ordering, roofing measurement integrations). It has zero whole-home concepts: no floorplans, no options engine, no design center, no community/lot/model abstraction. Cornerstone PM is a production home builder platform - 64 Designer Packages auto-locking category options, per-floorplan options reused across plan repeats, Structural vs Designer scope separation in bid templates, Area Cost Pricing for community extras, and 5 native AI agents (Foreman AI with 47 skills, Blueprint AI takeoff extracting 3,284+ parts in <60s, Bid Import AI, AI MLS Listing Generator, AI Support Agent). Frame as a respectful segment-mismatch post: AccuLynx is best-in-class for what it is; whole-home builders running 50+ houses/year across multiple communities need a different category of tool.
queue.heroImageAlt: AccuLynx alternative home builder software comparison with Cornerstone PM design center and floorplans

queue.title: Procore vs Cornerstone PM: Wrong Tool for Residential Builders?
queue.metaDescription: Procore is built for commercial GCs, not residential production builders. Compare Procore vs Cornerstone PM on design center, floorplans, and per-home options.
queue.angle: Procore markets to General Contractors, Owners, and Specialty Contractors with project-centric machinery (RFIs, submittals, drawings, project lifecycle management) tuned for $50M schools and $200M hospitals. Pricing is enterprise/quote-based, often tens of thousands annually with significant implementation. The data model is project-by-project - there is no concept of a Designer Package, per-floorplan options, plan repeats, model homes, community-assigned vendors, or buyer-facing design center. A production builder doing 80 homes/year does not need RFI workflows on a $400k single-family; they need the Magnolia plan set up once with 64 Designer Packages and sold 100 times across 5 communities. Position Procore as the right tool for commercial construction and the wrong tool for residential production - too much commercial machinery, no production-residential economy, no design center category at all.
queue.heroImageAlt: Procore alternative for residential home builders compared to Cornerstone PM production builder platform

queue.title: CoConstruct vs Cornerstone PM: Production Builder Design Center
queue.metaDescription: CoConstruct (now Buildertrend) builds for custom builders. Compare its job-level selections to Cornerstone PM's per-floorplan production design center model.
queue.primaryKeyword: production home builder design center software
queue.angle: CoConstruct's homepage now redirects to a 'CoConstruct + Buildertrend: Better together' migration page - they were acquired and customers are being moved to Buildertrend. Both products were built for custom builders: selections are job-level, negotiated per homeowner, treated as allowances. That is fundamentally the wrong model for a production builder. Cornerstone PM ships a true production design center: 64 curated Designer Packages across 7 categories that auto-lock category options when a package is picked, per-floorplan options priced once and reused across every home of that plan in every community, Structural vs Designer scope separation so bid templates show vendors only relevant scope, Option Classes tied to scope, Area Cost Pricing for community-level extras, and zero filler/default pricing - every dollar traces back to actual vendor pricing. CoConstruct has no Designer Package abstraction and no plan-level option model. The forced-migration story also adds churn anxiety: builders pushed to migrate once are open to migrating again to a platform actually built for their business model. This post owns the 'production design center' keyword and is the strongest custom-vs-production positioning piece in the queue.
queue.heroImageAlt: Production home builder design center software comparing CoConstruct job-level selections to Cornerstone PM Designer Packages

queue.title: Contractor Foreman vs Cornerstone PM for Production Home Builders
queue.metaDescription: Contractor Foreman starts at $49/month for small GCs. Compare it to Cornerstone PM's production home builder platform with AI takeoff and a design center.
queue.angle: Contractor Foreman markets itself as the most affordable PM software starting at $49/month with 1 user, targeting Subcontractors, Trade Contractors, and small Residential/Commercial GCs. Stack is Project Management + Financials + People (CRM-lite) + Documents + 50+ integrations (QuickBooks, CompanyCam, Stripe, Zapier, Kreo Takeoff as a separate purchase). It is a great tool for a 4-person GC running roofing, remodel, and small commercial out of a pickup truck. It is structurally underpowered for production home builders: no floorplan model, no options engine, no design center, no Designer Packages, no community/lot/model abstraction, no residential-aware takeoff, no Structural vs Designer scope split. A production builder doing 50-200 homes/year needs floorplan-level option pricing, multi-community vendor assignment, AI takeoff parsing CAD PDFs into 3,284+ parts, and a buyer-facing design center with locked package logic. Cornerstone is a different product category, not a more expensive Contractor Foreman - make that explicit and don't try to win on price.
queue.heroImageAlt: Contractor Foreman alternative for production home builders compared to Cornerstone PM design center

queue.title: Buildxact vs Cornerstone PM: Selections Software vs Design Center
queue.metaDescription: Buildxact is estimating-first with AI add-ons. Compare its selections workflow to Cornerstone PM's production home builder design center and bundled AI agents.
queue.primaryKeyword: home builder selections software vs design center
queue.angle: Buildxact is estimating-first for residential builders and remodelers, with pricing tiers Foundation $199/mo, Pro $399/mo, Master $599/mo, plus AI 'Blu' add-ons ($99-$149/mo each for Estimate Generator, Takeoff Assistant, Estimate Reviewer). Strength: fast quotes from takeoff to dealer pricing. Weakness for production builders: there is no Designer Package model, no per-floorplan options reused across plan repeats, no Structural vs Designer scope split, no community-assigned vendor wins, no buyer-facing design center, no Area Cost Pricing for community extras. Selections in Buildxact (and most custom-builder tools) are allowance-based per job. Cornerstone's design center is plan-level and community-aware: 64 Designer Packages auto-lock category options, the same Magnolia plan's options sell across 100 homes in 5 communities, and bid templates separate Structural and Designer scope so vendors see only their trade. AI is bundled (5 native AI agents: Foreman AI with 47 skills, Blueprint AI, Bid Import AI, MLS Listing Generator, Support Agent) - not $99/mo add-ons. This post owns the 'selections vs design center' keyword and reframes the conversation away from 'which estimating tool' toward 'which production-builder platform.'
queue.heroImageAlt: Home builder selections software vs design center comparing Buildxact estimating to Cornerstone PM Designer Packages

queue.title: JobNimbus vs Cornerstone PM: Home Builder Software Compared
queue.metaDescription: JobNimbus is a top roofing CRM trusted by 6,000+ contractors. Compare JobNimbus vs Cornerstone PM on floorplans, design center, and multi-community workflows.
queue.angle: JobNimbus is the '#1 Roofing CRM & Project Management Software' trusted by 6,000+ roofing businesses, with a stack of lead organization, sales automation, project boards, JobNimbus Payments, SumoQuote proposals, and QuickBooks/QXO/CompanyCam integrations. It is excellent for a roofer or exterior contractor scaling from $1M to $15M (their own case study). It is structurally the wrong tool for a whole-home builder: no floorplans, no design center, no Designer Packages, no options pricing engine, no community-lot-model abstraction, no multi-trade scope item architecture, and no residential takeoff parsing 130+ scopes from a CAD PDF. The 'production' JobNimbus references is roofing crew production, not home production. Cornerstone's 64 Designer Packages, per-floorplan options reused across plan repeats, Structural vs Designer scope separation, Area Cost Pricing, and community-assigned vendor wins are entire product layers JobNimbus has no equivalent for. Frame this as a respectful segment-mismatch post - like the AccuLynx comparison, JobNimbus is great for what it is and the audience for this post is the home builder who landed on JobNimbus because it kept showing up in 'construction CRM' lists.
queue.heroImageAlt: JobNimbus alternative home builder software comparing roofing CRM to Cornerstone PM production builder platform
